constructiveAlternatives.cost: Self-loops consume buffer and bandwidth like any blob - the real:cover ratio must be budgeted against the section-11 count-wall (more cover = fewer real blobs fit). Loop-return as reachability signal is noisy (eviction may kill your loop innocently). Self-addressed blobs trial-decrypt as 'mine' - tag them internally so they don't surface in the inbox UI.

constructiveAlternatives.title: Rateless fountain-coding each message into shards - anti-eviction, anti-suppression, and partial-deliverability-under-scale from one coding choice
constructiveAlternatives.addresses: Open problems (f) eviction steering a target out of the buffer, (g) hostile-relay suppression of a specific message-ID, deliverability past the section-11 count-wall, and seen-record robustness.
constructiveAlternatives.howItWorks: Today a message is ONE blob with ONE message-ID = hash(secret). That single ID is a single point of failure: a hostile relay that wants to suppress a known target ID can refuse to carry exactly it (open problem g), and adaptive eviction (f) only has to push ONE blob out of buffers. Instead, encode the 255-char payload with a rateless/fountain code (RaptorQ / LT, see arxiv 1308.0786 on fountain codes in opportunistic nets) into K identically-sized shards, each a normal sealed blob with its OWN derived ID; recipient needs any subset of ~K to reconstruct. Wins: (1) Suppression resistance: a censor must now recognize and drop ALL shards, but each shard's ID is an independent hash indistinguishable from any other blob - suppressing 'that message' requires breaking sealed-sender, so (g) largely dissolves. (2) Eviction resilience: a target message survives as long as ANY K-of-N shards survive anywhere in the component; random eviction (the spec's chosen policy) now has to get lucky N times, not once, raising the bar for (f). (3) Partial scale relief: at metropolis scale where buffers hold <1% of the soup, a flat single-blob message is all-or-nothing, but fountain shards degrade gracefully - even a fraction surviving the random walk gives non-zero reconstruction, softening the section-11 cliff (does NOT abolish it - still gathering-scale). (4) Anonymity: more blobs in flight per message = larger cover set.
constructiveAlternatives.benefit: One coding decision hardens THREE distinct attacks (suppression, eviction, scale-cliff) and grows the cover set. Fountain codes are mature and cheap to encode/decode. (Preserves all invariants: each shard is a standard fixed-size sealed blob, no routing, no destination, shard-index lives INSIDE the seal. One implementation landmine to watch: shard IDs must be mutually unlinkable on the wire - derive each from an independent ephemeral, not a visible sequence - or you create a correlation set.)
constructiveAlternatives.cost: N-fold blob multiplication per message directly worsens the section-11 count-wall (the binding constraint) - trades raw capacity for resilience; worth it only for user-marked high-stakes messages, or with small N (K=2-of-3). Reconstruction needs the recipient to collect a quorum, raising latency. Seen-record now tracks N IDs per message. Pollution attack (hostile malformed shards) defeated by verifying the inner signature BEFORE feeding the decoder (see the Lisbon RaptorQ-under-attack paper).

constructiveAlternatives.title: SOUL-BENDING (labeled): opt-in epoch-anchored RLN token for sustained-rate abuse - plus its soul-PRESERVING fallback
constructiveAlternatives.addresses: Open problem (a) anonymous rate-limiting - the one the spec calls the 'real lever' and 'unsolved.'
constructiveAlternatives.howItWorks: RLN (vac.dev/rln, PSE; RFC 32/RLN-V1) gives what the spec wants: a membership proof of 'I am SOME enrolled member' plus a per-epoch nullifier so the network detects when one member exceeds N messages/epoch (Shamir shares reconstruct and expose the over-sender's key). Strongest known fit for anonymous rate-limiting. BUT - why it's soul-bending - RLN canonically needs a shared Merkle membership tree (a registry/accumulator) all verifiers agree on. A global registry is a soft 'account/directory,' grazing invariants 1 (needs consensus to maintain), 6 (no account), arguably 7. SOUL-BENDING VARIANT: anchor the RLN tree to OUT-OF-BAND pairing - when two friends exchange keys (already happening) they co-enroll each other into a LOCAL per-social-graph accumulator; rate-limit proofs verifiable only by nodes sharing that branch. Keeps enrollment OOB (invariant 6-ish) and offline (invariant 1) but DOES add per-blob state partitioning verifiability by social graph, bending invariant 3's pure uniformity (a verifier in your branch can tell your blobs from a stranger's). Flagged honestly as not fully soul-preserving. SOUL-PRESERVING FALLBACK for the same problem: combine alternatives #1 and #2. The VDF (#1) makes SUSTAINED high-rate minting physically expensive in wall-clock time per device (the sequential floor a botnet can't parallelize away); rateless-sketch link caps (#2) bound per-neighbor injection. Together they convert 'rate-limiting' from 'meter an identity' (impossible without identity) to 'make sustained rate physically and locally bounded' (possible without identity) - the achievable shadow of the unsolved problem, requiring NO registry, NO consensus, NO account. Recommend shipping the fallback; keep RLN research-only, as the spec already files it.
constructiveAlternatives.benefit: Gives a concrete, buildable answer to the headline open problem WITHOUT a registry, by reframing rate-limiting as physical+local rather than identity-metered. The RLN variant is documented honestly so the team can reject it on principle rather than rediscover the conflict later. (The FALLBACK path - VDF + sketch caps - is the recommended one and preserves all invariants; only the labeled RLN variant bends invariants 1/3/6.)
constructiveAlternatives.cost: The honest cost: NO fully-soul-preserving option gives a true per-sender quota - that capability is fundamentally incompatible with invariants 3 and 4; the team should stop hoping for one. The fallback gives bounded sustained rate, not a quota. RLN variant's cost is the registry/consensus burden and uniformity erosion above.

creativeIdeas: STIGMERGIC SOFT-GRADIENT WITHOUT ROUTING (riskiest, highest-payoff, flagged): the section-11 scale-cliff exists because flooding has zero gradient, so a target blob random-walks and gets evicted before reaching a faraway friend. You cannot add a destination (invariant 2). But you CAN add a destination-AGNOSTIC gradient: let each blob carry an opaque 'demand counter' in the MUTABLE housekeeping region (already ignored for security) that increments when a node observes a neighbor 'want'-ing it in the sketch exchange (#2). Eviction then retains high-demand blobs preferentially - stigmergy / ant-trail reinforcement - pushing WIDELY-wanted blobs to survive longer without any node knowing WHO wants them or WHERE. CRUCIAL CAVEAT / why flagged: a recipient repeatedly 'want'-ing their own blob could inflate its counter, and a global observer correlating demand-counter growth with geography could partially reconstruct a gradient = soft routing metadata, grazing invariant 2/3. SOUL-PRESERVING TAMING: cap the counter, make increments probabilistic and rate-limited, RESET on each hop so it reflects only LOCAL instantaneous popularity not an accumulated path. Even tamed, needs formal traffic-analysis before trusting - research, not v1, but popularity-weighted random eviction (vs pure-random) is a real modest deliverability win that stays identity-agnostic.

MAKE THE COVER-TRAFFIC RATIO A PUBLISHED, AUDITABLE NUMBER, NOT A VIBE: the spec says 'quantify the real:dummy ratio and resulting k-anonymity set' but never picks one. Origination k-anonymity is literally (cover blobs)/(real blobs) in your emission window. Pin it as a first-class charge-independent floor (e.g. origination never more than 1-in-k of emissions, k>=16) and have the section-10 density-UX warning fire when local conditions force k below the floor. Converts anonymity from emergent hope into a measurable invariant with a UX contract - and it's free.

TTL AS A DELIVERABILITY DIAL THE USER ALREADY CONTROLS: section-11 notes storage proportional to TTL x rate, and the death-clock is sender-signed. Surface TTL in UX as 'reach vs footprint' (1 min = same-room only / maximally private / cheapest on the mesh; 7 days = best chance to reach a distant friend / largest footprint). Makes the user an active participant in the scale trade rather than a victim, and naturally biases the population toward short TTLs (which keeps the soup small enough that delivery works for everyone). The lever already exists; only the framing is missing.

DENIABLE DUAL-PASSPHRASE BUFFER (upgrade to panic/duress): the section-6 duress variant wipes+rotates, but a stronger primitive is two passphrases unlocking two distinct encrypted views of the buffer (one real, one innocuous-but-plausible), so a coerced unlock reveals a decoy soup. Standard deniable-encryption caveats apply (a sophisticated adversary knows the scheme exists), but against a checkpoint-grade adversary it strictly dominates wipe+rotate because it produces NON-EMPTY plausible content rather than a suspicious blank phone.

TREAT TRIAL-DECRYPT AS A BATCHED, PRECOMPUTED PIPELINE, NOT A PER-BLOB EVENT (retire open problem e with numbers): with one-time prekeys, 'is this mine?' is an X25519 + AEAD-tag check. Precompute the shared secrets for your outstanding prekeys ONCE; then trial-decrypt is a cheap symmetric tag check per blob. Combine with the seen-record (once per distinct ID) and the sketch layer (#2, only pull blobs you lack) and the per-blob crypto cost at volume becomes dominated by I/O, not ECDH. Worth an explicit v1 benchmark - it likely is NOT the bottleneck people fear, and saying so with measured numbers closes (e).

techniques.name: Wesolowski / Pietrzak Verifiable Delay Functions (and class-group VDFs needing no trusted setup)
techniques.relevance: Sequential, non-parallelizable proof-of-time that collapses the attacker mains-power/ASIC advantage from ~10^8x (PoW) to a small constant, AND yields a trustless age-floor hardening the absolute-TTL death guarantee. Refs: arxiv 2211.08162, eprint 2025/1782. Class-group variant avoids RSA ceremony - prefer it for an offline, trusted-setup-free system.

techniques.name: Rateless IBLT (SIGCOMM 2024, arxiv 2402.02668) and Minisketch (Blockstream / Bitcoin Erlay)
techniques.relevance: Set reconciliation in bandwidth proportional to the set DIFFERENCE not set size - the right replacement for the roadmap's Bloom have/want gossip, and simultaneously the principled per-neighbor abuse meter. Battle-tested in Bitcoin.

techniques.name: The Loopix Anonymity System (Piotrowska et al., USENIX Security 2017, arxiv 1703.00536)
techniques.relevance: Three Poisson streams (real / drop-cover / self-loop) provably indistinguishable; templates a concrete analyzable cover-traffic generator plus a free offline reachability sensor via loop-return. Formal anonymity analysis exists to borrow.

techniques.name: Rate-Limiting Nullifiers (PSE, vac.dev/rln; RFC 32/RLN-V1; Waku deployment)
techniques.relevance: Strongest documented anonymous rate-limiting primitive (Shamir + ZK membership + epoch nullifier). Honestly flagged as registry-dependent, hence soul-bending in this offline/no-account context; included so the team can reject on principle rather than rediscover the conflict.

techniques.name: RaptorQ / LT rateless fountain codes (RFC 6330; arxiv 1308.0786 fountain coding in opportunistic nets; di.fc.ul.pt RaptorQ-under-attack)
techniques.relevance: K-of-N shard encoding gives suppression-resistance (open problem g), eviction-resilience (f), and graceful scale-degradation (section-11) from one coding choice; the Lisbon paper documents pollution-attack hardening, defeated here by mandatory inner-signature-before-decode.

techniques.name: Bloom-filter epidemic forwarding / anti-entropy summary vectors in DTNs (arxiv 1208.3871; UOW epidemic-routing survey)
techniques.relevance: Establishes the baseline gossip mechanism the roadmap already assumes and its parameter-tuning literature - the thing rateless reconciliation (#2) upgrades. Confirms have/want exchange is not routing and stays inside invariant 2.

techniques.name: Stigmergy / ant-colony reinforcement (conceptual prior art; no single canonical citation claimed - marked uncertain)
techniques.relevance: Conceptual basis for the soft-gradient creative idea: emergent reinforcement of popular items without global addressing. Research-grade because its traffic-analysis safety in this setting is unproven and it grazes invariant 2 if not heavily tamed.

topRisks.title: Airtime, not storage, is the real scale wall — and the §11 math hides it
topRisks.why: The spec proves a 1–2GB buffer holds 250k–2M live blobs, but BLE has only 3 advertising channels with no hopping; at ~200 co-located advertisers/200ms collision probability is ~0.51 and a ~1KB blob is 5–7 chained PDUs or a GATT connection (Android ~7-conn cap). The soup that can physically CIRCULATE per minute is orders of magnitude below what the buffer holds, so 'your friend's blob survives in everyone's buffer → delivery works' does not follow. This is the load-bearing deliverability claim and it is unproven against radio physics.

topRisks.title: iOS background silently breaks uniformity AND reach
topRisks.why: A backgrounded iPhone drops its local name, forces service UUIDs into a 128-bit overflow bitmask readable only by explicitly-scanning iOS centrals, slows cadence, ignores scan dedup — it cannot emit a generic ID-free 1KB blob to Android at all. So an iPhone's wire profile is platform-distinguishable and near-silent in background, falsifying the charge-independent uniformity floor (Invariant 3) and removing ~half the crowd as relays (Invariant 1). It is currently an undocumented invariant violation that an adversary trivially partitions on.

topRisks.title: Anonymous rate-limiting candidates (RLN, blind tokens) all import forbidden infrastructure, and convergence-based detection is structurally blind here
topRisks.why: RLN needs an agreed membership Merkle tree, synchronized clocks, and slashable stake; blind tokens need an online issuer — all violate Invariants 1/2/6. Worse, every double-use detector (RLN Shamir reveal, offline e-cash) only fires when two conflicting proofs land in ONE observer's buffer, but the same §11 math that caps scale (each node holds <1% of the soup, constant partitions) guarantees non-convergence. The headline 'real lever' is not just missing infrastructure — its security proof assumes a global view this architecture can never have.

topRisks.title: First-emergence / first-spy provenance defeats 'no routing → no source' against a multi-sensor adversary
topRisks.why: In epidemic flooding the originator is the unique node emitting a novel message-ID with no prior reception. A cheap sensor mesh (a dozen Pi+BLE sniffers around a venue) timestamps each ID's first sighting and triangulates by RSSI to the first emitter. Cover traffic does not fix this — it localizes every blob equally. Combined with PHY-fingerprinting (CFO/IQ radiometrics, ~100% device ID in <1 min) and statistical-disclosure across repeated gatherings, the social graph of app users is reconstructable without decrypting a byte. The per-blob uniformity argument does almost no work against the persistent adversary the focus area demands.

topRisks.title: The product promise teaches a false belief the architecture cannot honor
topRisks.why: 'Leave no durable trace' / 'message in a bottle' is exactly the ephemerality claim research shows users over-trust into reckless behavior. The default deletes nothing recipient-side: screenshots, lock-screen notification mirrors, clipboard, keyboard learning, and any retained ciphertext (decryptable later on key seizure, §12.4 concedes). In a protest this false confidence gets people arrested — the precise bitchat marketing-vs-reality failure. The TTL is a real death guarantee for HONEST nodes only; the UX implies a universal one.

topRisks.title: TTL is denominated in wall-clock time the offline mesh cannot trust
topRisks.why: The death guarantee = local_clock ≥ creation_ts + TTL, but Invariant 1 means no NTP. An attacker runs a slow/backdated clock to become an immortal-blob reservoir re-seeding expired spam; honest phones whose RTC drifted (dead-battery reset in a blackout) silently extend everything they carry. creation_ts is signed by the (possibly malicious) sender, so it can be back/forward-dated. The entire §6 Knob-1 argument rests on synchronized honest clocks — an assumption never stated. This is the immortal-message bug one layer down.

topRisks.title: Eviction degrades to 'retain longest-TTL' under flood, re-creating the v0.1 bug
topRisks.why: With no routing there is no 'closest-to-delivery' signal, so 'retain-closest-to-delivery' collapses to 'retain-closest-to-TTL.' global-TTL is an attacker-chosen field (≤7d). A flooder stamps all junk TTL=7d; humane short-TTL real messages are always younger-relative-to-expiry and preferentially evicted, so the policy inverts to 'keep the longest-lived spam.' Random eviction blunts but does not remove this. Anti-abuse (random eviction) and deliverability (protect the real blob) are in direct, unresolved tension.

topRisks.title: Forward secrecy silently degrades to static-key fallback as the steady state
topRisks.why: X3DH one-time prekeys assume a server that atomically retires a used prekey and replenishes the batch. Serverless, a recipient hands a finite signed batch once at pairing and can never replenish offline; in a long blackout the static-key fallback is the NORMAL mode, not a corner case. No server enforces one-OTPK-per-message, so reuse/replay is undetectable, and the X3DH no-OTPK replay weakness applies directly (no Double Ratchet to close it). A seized static key then decrypts the entire fallback corpus — the exact harvest-now-decrypt-later loss FS was meant to prevent.

topRisks.title: Cold-start death spiral, amplified by the always-on cover floor
topRisks.why: Gathering-scope (Invariant 7) needs a crowd to be useful, but at launch there is none. First run: the app immediately emits PoW-valid cover blobs 24/7, drains battery, and delivers zero messages because no peers exist — cost up front, value never. The usual fix (server-backed single-user usefulness) is forbidden by Invariants 1/6. Anonymity-via-uniformity is also vacuous at low N (k=2 is deanonymization). This is a go-to-market death spiral the gathering invariant makes acute (you need a crowd in one place on day one).

topRisks.title: Seen-record Bloom is an attacker-controlled memory-DoS and silent-censorship surface
topRisks.why: message-ID = hash(secret) and secrets are free to mint off the send path. A flood of 10^8–10^9 distinct junk IDs forces every phone to insert each into its 30-day filter; past capacity the FP rate climbs toward 1.0 (silently dropping REAL inbound as 'already seen') or the filter must grow (OOM on the weakest battery phone = the defender). One data structure carries three conflicting jobs (anti-resurrection, replay defense, memory budget) and is sized for an honest ID population the attacker controls.

topRisks.title: PHY-layer radiometric fingerprinting nullifies the entire wire-anonymity stack
topRisks.why: The design demotes MAC randomization and relies on no-stable-tokens + payload rotation — none of which touches the radio. CFO/IQ-imbalance fingerprinting IDs a specific handset at ~100% in <1 min among hundreds of devices (Oakland '22/'24). The only real defense (active CFO obfuscation) is not in the design and needs chipset access most phones lack. Once every transmission carries a stable hardware label, MAC/payload rotation are moot and first-emergence + SDA compose into device-level deanonymization. Device-linkage should be assumed ≈1.0.

topRisks.title: OOB QR exchange is a supervised-MITM with no later detection path
topRisks.why: OOB forecloses the REMOTE MITM class but not an adversary controlling one endpoint (infiltrator, borrowed/seized phone, compromised display) presenting an indistinguishable key. With no server there is no key transparency (Trail of Bits' exact bitchat critique), so a fake-key attack is never detected later. Empirical ceremony completion is ~13–14% and meldingx leans on it harder than Signal with no fallback and silent unreadability on rotation. The 'forecloses MITM' claim is overstated and will be a CVE-class finding.

bestAlternatives.title: Rateless set reconciliation (Rateless IBLT / minisketch) over message-IDs as the gossip layer
bestAlternatives.solves: Airtime exhaustion (the #1 scale wall); trial-decrypt cost at volume (§13.5); per-neighbor abuse metering (§8); battery; eviction efficiency under churn.
bestAlternatives.sketch: Replace 'rebroadcast every blob' and naive Bloom have/want with: two neighbors exchange a compact sketch of their 256-bit blob-ID SETS and transfer only the symmetric difference (cost ∝ difference, not set size). Rateless so it adapts to variable-length BLE contacts (a 2s brush still makes progress; a long sit fully reconciles). Two neighbors sharing 1.99M of 2M blobs exchange ~thousands of IDs, not a megabyte. A neighbor whose sketch never decodes, or who 'wants everything' (a harvesting sink), is fast-droppable WITHOUT identity. Battle-tested in Bitcoin Erlay.
bestAlternatives.invariantsKept: All. Sketches are over opaque content-addressed IDs already public on the wire; reconciliation is symmetric and link-local; a have/want set is not a path or destination. No routing, no targeting, fully offline. (Wire framing of the sketch must be fixed-size / non-fingerprintable to keep Invariant 4 at the transport layer.)
bestAlternatives.tradeoff: More CPU (sketch decode) and protocol complexity than a Bloom filter; must bound decode attempts per neighbor (poisoning) and fall back to Bloom when the difference is huge (long disconnection).

bestAlternatives.title: Receive-before-originate gate + phone-local Poisson (Loopix) mixing of the outbound queue, with self-loops
bestAlternatives.solves: First-emergence/first-spy provenance (the worst anonymity attack); origination timing tell; PoW re-mine power side channel; the 'successful trial-decrypt = incoming real' inbox tell; offline reachability sensing; active-delete cover.
bestAlternatives.sketch: A phone NEVER originates a fresh real ID cold. It runs one outbound queue {relayed blobs, dummies, self-loops, real} popped at exponential (Poisson) inter-emission times, so timing reveals nothing about which blob is which. Before injecting a real origination it waits until it has recently relayed ≥k distinct NOVEL IDs, so the venue's first-sighting graph shows it constantly forwarding novelty — the real one is buried among them (gossip natural-DP improves with degree and relay depth). Self-loops sealed to your own key double as cover, as n-1/active-attack detection, and as a no-routing reachability sensor (loop returns ⇒ live mesh; never returns ⇒ percolation failure → powers the density UX).
bestAlternatives.invariantsKept: All. Self-loops are sealed to your OWN key (no third party); the gate uses only the relayed-novelty stream you already produce; pure flooding, no routing, no targeting, works offline.
bestAlternatives.tradeoff: Added origination latency (seconds-to-minutes in sparse venues) — degrades gracefully to a density-aware UX warning when novelty is scarce; cover/loop traffic consumes scarce airtime and buffer (must be budgeted against the count-wall).

bestAlternatives.title: Link-local, identity-free relay credentials spent INTO the link (rate-limit relaying, not origination)
bestAlternatives.solves: Anonymous rate-limiting (a) without RLN's forbidden tree/clock/stake and without convergence; connection/airtime flood DoS; eviction-under-flood; defender=weakest-battery (cost moves to the receive path you already named as real).
bestAlternatives.sketch: Stop bounding 'messages per global identity' (impossible without a global view). Each phone enforces a LOCAL rule: accept-and-relay at most R blobs per neighbor per neighbor-local epoch; to spend a slot, present a fresh, unlinkable single-use token verified cheaply but expensive to acquire. The token is consumed in the BLE handshake (never embedded in the blob), so the soup stays uniform and detection is trivially local (count tokens this epoch) — no convergence, no global clock (epoch = nonce exchanged at connection time). A flood now costs one scarce token PER HOP PER NEIGHBOR (compounds against propagation) instead of amortizing like a pre-mined origination pool.
bestAlternatives.invariantsKept: All. Tokens carry no identity and never go on the wire in the blob; epochs are neighbor-local (no global clock); no server, no registry; fully offline. Sealed-sender and uniformity intact.
bestAlternatives.tradeoff: Needs a Sybil-resistant token SOURCE (see next item); honest multi-hop messages spend more tokens than single-hop, so token economics must be calibrated so honest reach stays affordable; per-handshake verification adds a few ms.

bestAlternatives.title: Per-blob Verifiable Delay Function (sequential time-lock) replacing PoW, unifying mint-cost with a trustless age-floor
bestAlternatives.solves: PoW's 10^4–10^8x attacker compute asymmetry; congestion-coupled remote battery-drain; pre-mine amortization; clock-trust immortality / refresh-TTL (g/f); sustained-rate abuse (the soul-preserving shadow of (a)).
bestAlternatives.sketch: Require each blob to carry a Wesolowski/Pietrzak VDF proof (prefer class-group: no trusted setup) over its immutable core. A VDF is inherently SEQUENTIAL — a $10M ASIC beats a phone by a small constant, not 10^8x — collapsing the mains-power advantage. The iteration count is also a trustless wall-clock FLOOR: a blob proving N squarings could not exist more than N/(max-rate) ago, defending immortal-message/refresh-TTL structurally rather than by honest-clock convention. Mint cost and age-proof become one auditable artifact; cover blobs run the same VDF (uniformity preserved). Verify is cheap (ms); generation is the equalizer, done off the send path.
bestAlternatives.invariantsKept: All. The proof is a function of the blob's immutable core only — no endpoint identity, no routing, identical for real/cover/relayed; class-group params need no ceremony and run fully offline.
bestAlternatives.tradeoff: Proof generation costs real phone time/battery per minted blob (must be tuned so a 1-min 'next to me' message isn't gated by a 30s VDF); memory-hardness is lost (tiny-RAM ASIC fine — sequentiality is now the lever); N-device farms still get N sequential streams (bounds Sybils by device count, not human count).

bestAlternatives.title: Crypto-shred-on-read via puncturable encryption + decouple the delete token from message-ID
bestAlternatives.solves: Serverless prekey exhaustion/reuse (steady-state static fallback); the active-delete 'this was real' retroactive oracle (reveal of secret labels the blob); ack timing leak; intersection-attack ground-truth labels; coercion proof-of-authorship.
bestAlternatives.sketch: Two moves. (1) Replace one-time prekeys with puncturable encryption (Green-Miers, epoch-bucketed to bound key growth): ONE long-lived public key per identity (perfect for QR), reading a message punctures the key so it can never decrypt that ciphertext again — FS becomes the default with NO server, no batch to exhaust, no index to reuse. 'Disappear-after-read' is then a local key-puncture with ZERO wire signal. (2) For the OPTIONAL global delete, decouple the delete authority from the message-ID: message-ID = H(domain‖secret‖ephemeral_pk‖creation_ts) and delete-token = H(domain_delete‖secret), so revealing the delete-token purges without proving the original blob was real, and you can mint decoy deletes for your own cover (breaking the structural wall for the dummy population).
bestAlternatives.invariantsKept: All. Single static pubkey is OOB-exchanged as today; puncture is purely local (no beacon, honoring the no-recipient-signal rule); decoupled delete-token reveals nothing linkable to the wire ID. Soul-preserving: it stops SELLING 'erase from the world now' (unachievable vs retained ciphertext, §12.4 already concedes) and delivers 'unreadable on read + TTL death everywhere.'
bestAlternatives.tradeoff: Puncturable encryption is heavier than X25519 and key size grows with punctures (mitigate via per-epoch puncturing aligned to ≤7d TTL); must benchmark on weak phones (ties to open problem e). Loses true sender-fired global early-erase — keep the decoupled delete-token path if the product genuinely needs it.

bestAlternatives.title: Opportunistic-mobility ferrying + longer-range carriers (Wi-Fi Aware/NAN, optional LoRa) to beat the percolation cliff without internet or routing
bestAlternatives.solves: The percolation cliff / sparse-blackout reach (h, the ranked-#1 goal currently hand-waved to 'bridges or human ferries'); iOS-throttled effective degree; cover-traffic cost (NAN headroom).
bestAlternatives.sketch: (1) Make ferrying first-class and BLIND: a phone with no new blobs from any neighbor for T minutes (it has saturated its current island) preserves its soup more aggressively so it carries max payload to the next island — mobility-aware retention needing NO position, NO destination, just 'am I in a stale pocket,' plus a UX nudge ('you may be carrying messages out of range'). (2) Run the IDENTICAL uniform-soup over Wi-Fi Aware (~100m, ~100x neighbor-disk area, dodges iOS-BLE throttle) and, for rural blackout, an optional LoRa companion (km-range) — all carrying the same fixed-size sealed blobs as longer hops in the same flood. Both transports are opportunistic accelerators, never required.
bestAlternatives.invariantsKept: All (with one honest edge note). Ferrying carriers stay blind; same blobs, same trial-decrypt, no routing. The system MUST run BLE-only when NAN/LoRa is unavailable (mirroring the §9 internet-bridge philosophy), so the letter of Invariant 1 holds — NAN/LoRa are offline (no AP/internet). NAN's richer cluster-discovery metadata surface must be hardened to §10 and threat-modeled (OpenNAN MitM literature).
bestAlternatives.tradeoff: Ferry delivery is bounded by human movement (minutes-to-hours; useless for short TTL); NAN/LoRa are higher-power with their own fingerprinting surface; LoRa duty-cycle limits volume to low-rate long-TTL bridging and needs extra hardware; helps nothing if nobody moves between truly isolated islands.

bestAlternatives.title: Hop-energy decrement as a clock-independent SECONDARY death bound + gossip-median mesh-epoch clock
bestAlternatives.solves: Clock-skew immortality (the TTL-trust hole under the whole §6 argument); forged creation-ts; refresh-TTL.
bestAlternatives.sketch: Keep the signed absolute TTL as authoritative, but ADD an unauthenticated, monotonically-DECREMENTED energy counter in mutable housekeeping (born with budget B, every re-broadcast subtracts 1, floored at 0, never incremented). Energy 0 ⇒ local drop regardless of clock. This is the SAFE dual of forbidden reset-on-hop: decrement only ever shortens life (attacker-disincentivized), so it can be unauthenticated like other §5.1 housekeeping. Separately, each phone passively estimates a robust (trimmed-median) 'freshest creation-ts I've seen across many independent senders' from the trial-decrypt stream it already processes; a phone whose RTC disagrees by > threshold flags itself clock-untrusted and falls back to hop-energy for expiry.
bestAlternatives.invariantsKept: All. The energy scalar travels with the blob, identical for real/cover, adds ~2 bytes (constant size, uniformity intact), no routing. The clock estimator is passive over data already on the wire — no new messages, no identity, no targeting, fully offline.
bestAlternatives.tradeoff: B sizing is a tuning risk (too low partitions early, too high is toothless); a forward-dated-ts flood could pull the median forward (early honest expiry) — mitigate with a low quantile and clamping drift to plausible RTC rates.

bestAlternatives.title: Truth-in-labeling UX + mutual-presence pairing ceremony (mutual QR + emoji SAS) + honest send/trust states
bestAlternatives.solves: False-confidence harm (the protest-arrest failure); supervised-MITM with no detection; silent rotation failure; ignored tap-through warnings.
bestAlternatives.sketch: (1) Drop 'leave no durable trace'; lead with the honest metaphor: 'We hide WHO threw the bottle and WHO it's for. We can't stop the finder from keeping it.' Ship a default-on first-run 'what this does/does NOT protect' card set and per-feature inline honesty (TTL picker: 'The person you send to can keep it'). (2) Replace one-way QR scan with a single 'hold phones together' gesture: mutual QR + a 3-emoji/4-word SAS both humans glance-confirm (far higher completion than 13–14% digit comparison), optionally binding a spoken low-entropy shared word into the transcript. (3) Persistent local trust states {Verified-in-person, Unverified, Key-changed!}; key mismatch quarantines messages behind a tap and says 'verify in person' rather than silently dropping; send states say 'Released to the soup,' 'No peers nearby — holding,' 'Expired before any peer' — never a false 'delivered.'
bestAlternatives.invariantsKept: All. No key material ever leaves the OOB channel; trust UI and SAS are purely local; nothing new on the wire; works fully offline.
bestAlternatives.tradeoff: A less magical marketing pitch and some onboarding friction (the friction is the point); badge-blindness risk (reserve red strictly for key-change); pairing needs both phones+cameras present (already the OOB assumption).

bestAlternatives.title: Fountain/rateless coding of each high-stakes message into K-of-N uniform shards
bestAlternatives.solves: Hostile-relay suppression of a known target ID (g); adaptive eviction steering one blob out of buffers (f); graceful (partial) degradation past the §11 scale-cliff; grows the cover set.
bestAlternatives.sketch: Encode the 255-char payload with RaptorQ/LT into K identically-sized shards, each a normal sealed blob with its OWN independently-derived ID; recipient reconstructs from any ~K-of-N. A censor must now recognize and drop ALL shards, but each shard's ID is an independent hash indistinguishable from any other blob, so suppression requires breaking sealed-sender. Random eviction must get lucky N times, not once. At metropolis scale where buffers hold <1% of the soup, a flat single-blob message is all-or-nothing whereas shards degrade gracefully. Verify the inner signature BEFORE feeding the decoder to defeat pollution shards.
bestAlternatives.invariantsKept: All, with one implementation landmine: each shard is a standard fixed-size sealed blob, no routing, shard-index INSIDE the seal — but shard IDs must be mutually unlinkable on the wire (derive each from an independent ephemeral, not a visible sequence) or you create a correlation set.
bestAlternatives.tradeoff: N-fold blob multiplication worsens the airtime/count-wall (the binding constraint), so use small N (e.g. 2-of-3) and only for user-marked high-stakes messages; recipient needs a quorum (higher latency); seen-record tracks N IDs per message.

bestAlternatives.title: Sliding-window / aging seen-record (cuckoo+timestamp or two-generation A2) replacing the monolithic 30-day Bloom
bestAlternatives.solves: Seen-record memory-DoS and false-positive censorship; need for exact deletion (panic-purge); §13.8.
bestAlternatives.sketch: The seen-record only needs to outlive max-TTL (7d) with margin, not accumulate 30 flat days. Use a sliding-window Bloom (cuckoo storing fingerprint+timestamp) or a two-generation A2 active-aging filter: at capacity, age out the OLDEST generation rather than letting FP climb toward 1.0. Anti-resurrection becomes 'an ID cannot be re-accepted within W, and W is provably ≥ maxTTL+margin even under maximal flood because aging is FIFO-by-time, not FP-by-saturation.' The cuckoo variant supports exact deletion for panic-wipe. Tie per-ID retention to that ID's own signed TTL so short-TTL floods cost less memory.
bestAlternatives.invariantsKept: All. Pure local data-structure swap; no wire change, no identity, no routing.
bestAlternatives.tradeoff: ~2x the bits of a space-optimal Bloom and more complex; insertion can fail (needs a stash); must formally prove window-under-flood ≥ maxTTL (now a concrete inequality).

bestAlternatives.title: Density-adaptive auto-knobs: cover floor, relay floor, and token price all keyed to LOCALLY-observed density
bestAlternatives.solves: Relay-retention tragedy-of-the-commons (b) without identity; sparse-vs-dense tension (relay generously when starving, throttle when dense); cold-start battery-for-nothing; the unactionable density warning.
bestAlternatives.sketch: Make the globally-optimal behavior also the locally-rational default, as a pure function of first-hand observation (count distinct PEER radios, buffer pressure) identical for every honest node. When density is HIGH the commons is healthy → lower your own retention/relay floor and RAISE token price (throttle floods where they're possible). When density is LOW (near the cliff) → raise retention to the max and demand ~0 token price (reach is precious). This removes the battery-vs-altruism conflict, can't be remotely spiked by a lying relay (unlike congestion-coupled PoW), and degrades to free generous relaying in exactly the blackout case ranked #1. At N≈0, drop cover to a cheap standby (k-anonymity is vacuous with no crowd to hide in) and tell the user honestly.
bestAlternatives.invariantsKept: All if density is estimated from hard-to-forge signals (distinct PHY peer radios, not distinct blobs) and auto-lowering is clamped. Identical rule for all nodes ⇒ no per-device tell; no identity, no routing.
bestAlternatives.tradeoff: Density estimate is gameable (an attacker emitting many distinct radios/IDs can fake 'dense' to suppress honest traffic, or fake 'sparse'); a smooth ramp (not a step) is needed to avoid a standby→active transition tell; less powerful than true reciprocity (no reward for the generous).

crossCutting: Five 'separate' open problems share ONE root cause: the wire carries no state a node can reason about except 'this is a fixed-size blob.' Anti-flood is unsolved because there is no identity to meter; relay incentives are unsolved because there is no identity to reward; cover realism is hard because there is no shared notion of 'normal'; deliverability craters because flooding has no gradient and the buffer is a flat bag; eviction is hard because blobs have no trust axis. The high-leverage move is to add ONE class of identity-free, blob-or-neighborhood-scoped signal (set-reconciliation sketches, link-local tokens, VDF age, density observation) that feeds all five at once — never an endpoint-correlated signal.

The deepest tension is anonymity-via-uniformity vs every form of discrimination the system needs to survive. Uniformity forbids dropping 'cover-shaped' blobs (so cover is a sanctioned flood vector), forbids stable neighbor identity (so per-neighbor caps are Sybil-bypassable via MAC churn), and forbids per-message scrutiny (so abuse and delivery cannot be told apart). The resolution running through the best alternatives: move discrimination OFF the wire and into LINK-LOCAL, EPHEMERAL, PER-SESSION decisions (token spend in the handshake, sketch decode, per-session airtime ranking) that leave no durable per-peer state and no on-wire metadata.

Per-blob uniformity is true but does NOT deliver what users think. The honest, defensible claim is ORIGINATION-EVENT indistinguishability within a spatiotemporal anonymity set of size K — and K must become a measured, published, UX-surfaced number ('your message will be hidden among ~37 origination events'), with K = cover-originations / real-originations as a first-class charge-independent floor. Three findings independently converge on this reframing; it is the single most important conceptual correction.

Assume device-linkage ≈ 1.0. PHY radiometric fingerprinting (CFO/IQ) survives MAC and payload rotation, so MAC demotion + 'no stable tokens' protect only against a cheap passive sniffer (which the spec concedes). Every higher-layer defense must be designed to be safe-ish even when the transmitter is uniquely labeled — which forces the K-anonymity-of-origination-events framing, because it is the only property that survives perfect device linkage. Run a USRP self-audit and publish the number rather than assuming it bounded.

The optional active-delete spends real, irreversible anonymity budget (a retroactive 'this blob was real' oracle via reveal-of-secret, plus an intersection-attack ground-truth label) to buy a guarantee it cannot deliver — a hostile relay simply ignores the delete and keeps the ciphertext. Cost lands on the honest case; benefit does not land on the adversarial case. The right move is not decoy-deletes chasing the unwinnable structural wall, but to change what 'delete' IS: crypto-shred-on-read (zero wire signal) for the common case, and decouple the delete-token from the message-ID for the rare global case. Default active-delete OFF and label it 'this emits a detectable signal.'

The internet-bridge philosophy in §9 ('opportunistic accelerator, refuse rather than downgrade, tested with internet disabled') is the correct template for THREE other subsystems: Wi-Fi Aware/LoRa (opportunistic range, BLE-only must still work), the standby cover floor (relax only when provably no crowd to hide in), and active-delete (optional, off by default, honestly labeled). Generalize it into a design rule: every capability that bends an invariant must be an opt-in accelerator that the BLE-only / offline / uniform core never depends on.

Honesty is the product's strongest asset AND its biggest unshipped risk. The design doc is unusually candid (§11/§12.2/§12.5, 'good crypto is not enough'), but honesty in a doc is not honesty in the running app — the gap between what §12 admits and what a user holding the app believes is where people get hurt. Truth-in-labeling first-run UX, honest send states ('Released to the soup', never 'delivered'), and an in-app 'red-team reality' mode convert the limitations into trust and pre-empt the inevitable researcher-dunk cycle. This is cheap (copywriting + a few screens) and high-impact.

The bootstrap problem is a UX/go-to-market problem misfiled as percolation physics. The unit of adoption that matches Invariant 7 is a PLANNED GATHERING, not metropolis virality: organizer 'gathering kits' (poster QR → pre-shared event contact bundle), pair-to-activate (useful at N=2), and a standby mode that doesn't burn battery at N=0. You don't need a city; you need 200 people in one square on one afternoon, which is an organizing problem with known solutions.

Several proposed fixes COMPOSE into a coherent stack rather than competing: rateless reconciliation (#1) carries the link-local tokens (#3) and surfaces the reconciled 'newcomer' delta that freshness-priority eviction protects; Loopix Poisson mixing (#2) provides the cover floor that the density-adaptive controller (density auto-knobs) tunes and whose self-loops feed the reachability sensor that powers the honest density UX; VDF age-floor (#4) backstops the hop-energy + gossip-median clock; puncturable-encryption crypto-shred (#5) removes the wire-delete substrate that the anonymity attacks feast on. The roadmap should sequence them so each lands on the layer the next depends on.

The single hardest residual tension that no soul-preserving option fully resolves: a true PER-SENDER QUOTA is fundamentally incompatible with Invariants 3 and 4 (you cannot meter an identity you refuse to carry). The achievable shadow is 'sustained rate made physically expensive (VDF) and locally bounded (link tokens + per-neighbor caps),' which bounds rate without a quota. The team should stop hoping for a quota and design to the achievable shadow; RLN-on-the-social-graph is the only thing that gives a true quota and it bends Invariants 1/3/6, so keep it research-only and labeled.

roadmap.step: PHASE 0 — Re-scope the headline claims and ship an AIRTIME-budget table beside the §11 storage table
roadmap.rationale: The biggest risk (airtime, not storage, is the scale wall) is currently invisible because the spec only proves the buffer fits the soup, not that the radio can stir it. Compute live-blobs-that-can-CIRCULATE-per-minute given 3 adv channels, collision-vs-density, and chosen cadence. Simultaneously reframe the public claim from per-blob uniformity to 'origination-event K-anonymity' and from 'leave no durable trace' to the honest bottle metaphor. This sets the real scope and prevents building on falsified assumptions; costs analysis + copywriting, not architecture.

roadmap.step: PHASE 1 — Land the highest-ROI, all-invariant-preserving wins: rateless set reconciliation; mutual-presence pairing + truth-in-labeling UX; honest send/trust states
roadmap.rationale: Rateless IBLT/minisketch is a drop-in for an already-planned component that simultaneously fixes the airtime wall, trial-decrypt cost, battery, and gives anti-abuse a principled hook — four open items, one battle-tested library, zero invariant cost. The pairing ceremony + truth-in-labeling are pure-local UX that close the supervised-MITM and false-confidence harms (the two most likely to get a user arrested) for the price of copy and a few screens. These are the cheapest, safest, highest-impact moves.

roadmap.step: PHASE 2 — Add the anti-flood + anonymity primitives: link-local relay tokens (spent into the handshake), Poisson outbound mixing with self-loops, and the receive-before-originate gate
roadmap.rationale: These attack the three critical structural problems that survived Phase 1 — anonymous rate-limiting (without RLN's forbidden infrastructure), first-emergence provenance, and connection/airtime flood DoS — all while preserving every invariant. They depend on Phase 1's reconciliation layer (tokens ride the handshake; the gate consumes the reconciled novelty stream), so they sequence after it. Self-loops also bootstrap the reachability sensor needed for the density UX.

roadmap.step: PHASE 3 — Harden the death guarantee and storage: hop-energy decrement + gossip-median clock; sliding-window/aging seen-record; youngest-by-real-age eviction; density-adaptive auto-knobs
roadmap.rationale: With abuse and anonymity addressed, close the LIFETIME holes that an attacker exploits once flooding is partially contained: clock-skew immortality, seen-record memory-DoS/censorship, TTL-field eviction steering, and the relay tragedy-of-the-commons. All are local data-structure or policy changes preserving invariants. The density auto-knobs reuse the peer-counting the gate already needs and resolve the sparse-vs-dense tension.

roadmap.step: PHASE 4 — Tackle the percolation cliff and the cold-start spiral: blind mobility-aware ferrying, opportunistic Wi-Fi Aware/LoRa carriers, organizer 'gathering kits' + pair-to-activate + standby mode
roadmap.rationale: The ranked-#1 goal (works in a blackout) is currently hand-waved to 'bridges or human ferries.' Engineer ferrying as a first-class blind retention policy and promote NAN/LoRa as opportunistic longer hops running the identical soup (BLE-only must still work). Pair this with the go-to-market fix (planned-gathering adoption unit) so the app is useful from N=2 and doesn't burn battery at N=0. These are larger lifts and benefit from the earlier layers being stable.

roadmap.step: PHASE 5 — Re-derive the crypto KEY-MANAGEMENT layer for a serverless world: puncturable-encryption crypto-shred-on-read; decoupled delete-token; X-Wing hybrid (not a hand-rolled combiner) with key-committing AEAD; deniable MAC authenticator
roadmap.rationale: The envelope crypto is sound but key management was specified for a client-server world; this is the deepest correctness lift and should be done deliberately with benchmarks on weak phones (trial-decrypt cost, puncture cost), not rushed. Crypto-shred makes FS the default and removes the wire-delete substrate the anonymity attacks exploit; X-Wing fixes the ML-KEM non-binding trial-decrypt ambiguity before PQ lands; deniable MAC restores coercion-deniability the seizure threat model needs. Treat the bucketing decision (classical vs hybrid envelope size) as a flag-day, never a mixed population.

roadmap.step: PHASE 6 — Stand up continuous verification: multi-OS transport conformance harness; adversarial eviction CI gate; USRP PHY self-audit; internet-disabled CI (extend §9)
roadmap.rationale: The uniformity-floor, reach, and eviction claims must be continuously verified on real silicon and against modeled attackers, not assumed. A multi-OEM + iOS-foreground/background + soup-brick harness measures real circulation and iOS emission gaps; the eviction CI gate operationalizes §13.6 as a regression test; the USRP audit publishes the real device-linkability number that justifies the K-anonymity framing. Research-only items (fountain shards, RLN-on-social-graph, stigmergic soft-gradient) stay flagged and out of the shipping core.

synthesis.soulCheck: "The soul still coheres — and notably, it gets STRONGER under this review rather than being dismantled. Pure flooding, anonymity-via-uniformity, sealed-sender, absolute-TTL self-destruct, no-server/OOB identity, and gathering-scope are all the right calls; every one of the strongest alternatives preserves all seven invariants, and several (VDF age-floor strengthening TTL, self-loops strengthening cover, set-reconciliation strengthening uniformity-at-the-transport, crypto-shred strengthening FS, truth-in-labeling strengthening the threat-model honesty) make the invariants MORE robust than the v0.2 spec. The design's defining honesty is its best feature. But there are two things to face squarely. FIRST, the single biggest UNSOLVED thing is not a crypto gap — it is that the two ranked-highest goals are in direct, unresolved physical tension with the chosen mechanism: 'works in a total blackout' (Invariant 1, ranked #1) and 'pure flooding for anonymity' (Invariant 2) collide at the PERCOLATION CLIFF, where below a critical mean degree the network shatters into islands that never exchange a blob and flooding cannot cross a gap it cannot bridge — and the only honest fixes (blind human ferrying, opportunistic longer-range radios) are probabilistic, slow, and degrade to nothing in true isolation. Anonymous rate-limiting (Invariant 2 vs the need to meter without identity) is the celebrated unsolved problem, but it has an achievable shadow (physical + link-local bounding); the percolation cliff in a sparse blackout WITHOUT internet or routing has NO clean answer, only mitigations that buy probability, not a guarantee. SECOND, and more sobering: even with every fix above, the persistent wide-coverage sensor-net + PHY-fingerprinting adversary that the focus area demands we confront is not defeated, only made more expensive — device-linkage should be assumed ≈1.0, and the most defensible promise is 'an attacker cannot deny service to or deanonymize friend-to-friend traffic in a dense gathering without physically deploying O(crowd-size) radios and sensors.' That is a real, falsifiable, honorable guarantee — but it is narrower than the marketing instinct wants, and stating it plainly (in the app, not just the doc) is the act that keeps the soul intact."
